   Learn how to install custom resource definitions (CRDs) and configure the Helm chart so that you can run Consul API Gateway on your Kubernetes deployment.

# Enable standard API gateway for Kubernetes



The Consul API gateway ships with Consul and standard API Gateway controller is automatically installed when you install Consul on Kubernetes.
Consul from version 2.0 introduces the new API Gateway controller and the new API Gateway CRDs under the API Group `consul.hashicorp.com`.

The Standard API Gateway controller is installed by default and is the recommended way to deploy the API gateway under the API Group `gateway.networking.k8s.io`.

The Consul API Gateway controller is installed when you enable the `consulapi` CRD in the `values.yaml` file. The Consul API Gateway controller is recommended only under the following condition:
  1. When the user is using TCPRoute associated to the API Gateway and Gateway CRDs are managed by the platform under version v1 , under API group `gateway.networking.k8s.io` and not supporting TCPRoute CRD installation.


Before you begin the installation process, verify that the environment you are deploying Consul and the API gateway in meets the requirements listed in the [Technical Specifications](/consul/docs/north-south/api-gateway/k8s/tech-specs). Refer to the [Release Notes](/consul/docs/release-notes) for any additional information about the version you are deploying.

1. The Consul Helm chart deploys the API gateway using the configuration specified in the `values.yaml` file. Refer to [Helm Chart Configuration - `connectInject.apiGateway`](/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#apigateway) for information about the Helm chart configuration options. Create a `values.yaml` file for configuring your Consul API gateway deployment and include the following settings:

  <Tabs>

    <Tab heading="Reference configuration">
    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
      name: consul
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP <=4.18)">

    On an OpenShift Kubernetes cluster, user must include the `global.openShift.enabled` parameter and set it to `true`. Refer to [OpenShift requirements](/consul/docs/connect/gateways/api-gateway/tech-specs#openshift-requirements) for additional information.

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
       openshift:
         enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: true
       cni:
         enabled: true
         logLevel: info
         multus: true
         cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
         cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP >=4.19)">

    On an OpenShift Kubernetes cluster, user must include the `global.openShift.enabled` parameter and set it to `true`. Refer to [OpenShift requirements](/consul/docs/connect/gateways/api-gateway/tech-specs#openshift-requirements) for additional information. Furthermore, user may choose to use the new Kubernetes resource types in the `consul.hashicorp.com` API resource group. These types provide additional support for Consul on OpenShift Container Platform (OCP). For guidance, refer to the related [resource types upgrade procedure](/consul/docs/north-south/api-gateway/k8s/resource-upgrade).

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       openshift:
         enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: true
       cni:
         enabled: true
         logLevel: info
         multus: true
         cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
         cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="GKE Autopilot">

    By default, GKE Autopilot installs [Gateway API resources](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io), so we recommend customizing the `connectInject.apiGateway` stanza to accommodate the pre-installed Gateway API CRDs.

    The following working example enables both Consul Service Mesh and Consul API Gateway on GKE Autopilot. Refer to [`connectInject.agiGateway` in the Helm chart reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#v-connectinject-apigateway) for additional information.

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       name: consul
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: false
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     server:
       resources:
         requests:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
         limits:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="Kubernetes Clusters with Gateway CRDs managed by cluster platforms">

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       name: consul
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: false
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>
  </Tabs>

1. Install Consul API Gateway using the standard Consul Helm chart or Consul K8s CLI specify the custom values file. Refer to the [Consul Helm chart](https://github.com/hashicorp/consul-k8s/releases) in GitHub releases for the available versions.

  <Tabs>
  <Tab heading="Consul K8s CLI (Mac Only)">
  
  Refer to the official [Consul K8S CLI documentation](/consul/docs/reference/cli/consul-k8s) to find additional settings.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ brew tap hashicorp/tap
  ```
  
  ```shell-session
  $ brew install hashicorp/tap/consul-k8s
  ```
  
  ```shell-session
  $ consul-k8s install -config-file=values.yaml -set global.image=hashicorp/consul:1.17.0
  ```
  
  </Tab>
  <Tab heading="Helm">
  
  Add the HashiCorp Helm repository.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ helm repo add hashicorp https://helm.releases.hashicorp.com
  ```
  
  Install Consul with API Gateway on your Kubernetes cluster by specifying the `values.yaml` file.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ helm install consul hashicorp/consul --version 1.3.0 --values values.yaml --create-namespace --namespace consul
  ```
  
  </Tab>
  </Tabs>



# Enable Consul API gateway for Kubernetes

The Consul API gateway using `consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1` provides native Consul integration and ships with Consul. It is optionally available when you install Consul on Kubernetes. Before you begin the installation process, verify that the environment you are deploying Consul and the Consul API gateway in meets the requirements listed in the [Technical Specifications](/consul/docs/north-south/api-gateway/k8s/tech-specs). Refer to the [Release Notes](/consul/docs/release-notes) for any additional information about the version you are deploying.

1. The Consul Helm chart deploys the Consul API gateway using the configuration specified in the `values.yaml` file. Refer to [Helm Chart Configuration - `connectInject.apiGateway`](/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#apigateway) for information about the Helm chart configuration options. Create a `values.yaml` file for configuring your Consul API gateway deployment and include the following settings:

  <Tabs>

    <Tab heading="Reference configuration">
    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
    global:
      installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
      name: consul
      crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: true
    connectInject:
      enabled: true
      apiGateway:
        manageExternalCRDs: true
        manageNonStandardCRDs: true
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP <=4.18)">

    If you are installing Consul on an OpenShift Kubernetes cluster version 4.18 or earlier, you must include the `global.openShift.enabled` parameter and set it to `true`. Refer to [OpenShift requirements](/consul/docs/connect/gateways/api-gateway/tech-specs#openshift-requirements) for additional information.

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: true
       openshift:
         enabled: true
         crds:
          consulapi:
            enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: true
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
       cni:
         enabled: true
         logLevel: info
         multus: true
         cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
         cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="OpenShift (OCP >=4.19)">

    If you are installing Consul on an OpenShift Kubernetes cluster version 4.19 or later, you must include the `global.openShift.enabled` parameter and set it to `true`. Refer to [OpenShift requirements](/consul/docs/connect/gateways/api-gateway/tech-specs#openshift-requirements) for additional information. Furthermore, if your OpenShift cluster is version 4.19 or later, you may choose to use new Kubernetes resource types in the `consul.hashicorp.com` API resource group. These types provide additional support for Consul on OpenShift Container Platform (OCP). For guidance, refer to the related [resource types upgrade procedure](/consul/docs/north-south/api-gateway/k8s/resource-upgrade).

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       openshift:
         enabled: true
         crds:
          consulapi:
            enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: true
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
       cni:
         enabled: true
         logLevel: info
         multus: true
         cniBinDir: "/var/lib/cni/bin"
         cniNetDir: "/etc/kubernetes/cni/net.d"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="GKE Autopilot">

    By default, GKE Autopilot installs [Gateway API resources](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io), so we recommend customizing the `connectInject.apiGateway` stanza to accommodate the pre-installed Gateway API CRDs while enabling the Standard API gateway resources.

    The following working example enables both Consul Service Mesh and Consul API Gateway on GKE Autopilot. Refer to [`connectInject.agiGateway` in the Helm chart reference](https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/docs/reference/k8s/helm#v-connectinject-apigateway) for additional information.

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

     ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       name: consul
       crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: false
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     server:
       resources:
         requests:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
         limits:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

    <Tab heading="Kubernetes Clusters with Gateway CRDs managed by cluster platforms">

    <CodeBlockConfig filename="values.yaml">

    ```yaml
     global:
       installK8sNetworkingCRDs: false
       name: consul
       crds:
        consulapi:
          enabled: true
     connectInject:
       enabled: true
       apiGateway:
         manageExternalCRDs: false
         manageNonStandardCRDs: true
     server:
       resources:
         requests:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
         limits:
           memory: "500Mi"
           cpu: "500m"
     ```
    </CodeBlockConfig>
    </Tab>

  </Tabs>

1. Install Consul API Gateway using the standard Consul Helm chart or Consul K8s CLI specify the custom values file. Refer to the [Consul Helm chart](https://github.com/hashicorp/consul-k8s/releases) in GitHub releases for the available versions.

  <Tabs>
  <Tab heading="Consul K8s CLI (Mac Only)">
  
  Refer to the official [Consul K8S CLI documentation](/consul/docs/reference/cli/consul-k8s) to find additional settings.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ brew tap hashicorp/tap
  ```
  
  ```shell-session
  $ brew install hashicorp/tap/consul-k8s
  ```
  
  ```shell-session
  $ consul-k8s install -config-file=values.yaml -set global.image=hashicorp/consul:1.17.0
  ```
  
  </Tab>
  <Tab heading="Helm">
  
  Add the HashiCorp Helm repository.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ helm repo add hashicorp https://helm.releases.hashicorp.com
  ```
  
  Install Consul with Consul API Gateway on your Kubernetes cluster by specifying the `values.yaml` file.
  
  ```shell-session
  $ helm install consul hashicorp/consul --version 1.3.0 --values values.yaml --create-namespace --namespace consul
  ```
  
  </Tab>
  </Tabs>



# API gateway reconciliation

Starting with Consul 2.0, Consul only reconciles API gateway resources that meet specific requirements. For Consul to manage an API gateway, the gateway must:

1. Include the required labels in the gateway metadata
2. Specify the correct controller name that matches the gateway type

Consul validates both criteria before reconciling the gateway resource.

## Standard gateway

For the standard Kubernetes Gateway API (using `gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1`), the gateway must:
- Set the `gatewayClassName` to reference a GatewayClass with controller name `consul.hashicorp.com/gateway-controller`
- Include the label `component: api-gateway` in the metadata

Example configuration:

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: api-gateway # can be any
  namespace: consul
  labels:
    component: api-gateway # should be this
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

## Consul API Gateway

For the  Consul Gateway resource (using `consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1`), the gateway must:
- Set the `gatewayClassName` to reference a GatewayClass with controller name `consul.hashicorp.com/gateway-controller-consul`
- Include the label `component: api-gateway-consul` in the metadata

Example configuration:

<CodeBlockConfig>

```yaml
apiVersion: consul.hashicorp.com/v1beta1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: api-gateway-consul # can be any
  namespace: consul
  labels:
    component: api-gateway-consul # should be this
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
